name: TAO Retest Hold Derisk v1
edge_mechanism: retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing whether retest-hold continuation can work if the trade is explicitly de-risked by time whenever expansion does not appear quickly. The edge should come from participating only after structure survives the retest, while conceding early when the market refuses to expand.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-hold setups are too sparse, if continuation still fails after quick de-risking, or if the lane is too noisy for the retest structure to remain interpretable.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A prior directional move retests the breakout zone, holds above fast value, and either expands promptly or gets cut early when momentum does not appear.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy markets often kill continuation late. A retest-hold entry with fast time-based de-risking may keep only the subset that expands promptly enough to pay costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because retest behavior and expansion amplitude are large enough for time-based de-risking to matter materially.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If this lane only produces noisy retests with no orderly second leg, the quick de-risk will reduce damage but not create edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the retest idea is right but the time stop is still too slow, shorten de-risk timing before touching the retest grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A manageable number of retest continuations where many weak trades get cut quickly and the winners expand early.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h and one cross-lane show enough density and better containment of failed continuations through time-based de-risking.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse participation, or no measurable benefit from the fast de-risk structure.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest, hold, and time-based de-risk are compatible
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest-hold definitions rather than exact breakout-candle choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best stress-test lane for retest-hold continuation with fast de-risking.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest-hold setups show enough density and weak trades are contained early.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup is too sparse or quick de-risking adds no measurable value.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A moderate set of retest continuations where losers get cut fast and winners expand promptly.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same retest-hold grammar survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows similar continuation with less noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only appears on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller retest continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the de-risk logic transfers to a cleaner control lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC preserves the retest-hold mechanism with measurable benefit from faster de-risking.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More orderly retest continuations than TAO with lower amplitude.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
